messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
Which movie did Samuel L. Jackson win a BAFTA award for best supporting actor in 1994

content.message:
To determine which movie Samuel L. Jackson won a BAFTA award for best supporting actor in 1994, I will proceed step by step:

1. Identify the entity "Samuel L. Jackson" in Wikidata.
2. Find the properties related to awards or BAFTA nominations or wins.
3. Filter the results for "best supporting actor" and the year 1994.
4. Identify the movie associated with that award.

First, I'll search for the entity "Samuel L. Jackson" in Wikidata to get his identifier.

tool_calls.name: search_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 entity alternatives:
1. Samuel L. Jackson (wd:Q172678), also known as "Sam Jackson", "Sam Leroy Jackson", "Sam L Jackson", "Sam L. Jackson":
  - American actor (born 1948)
  - has occupation actor
  - has occupation character actor
  - has occupation film actor
  - has occupation film producer
  - has occupation television actor
  - has occupation television producer
  - has occupation voice actor
  - is instance of human
2. Samuel Lewis Jackson (wd:Q130435851, matched via "Samuel L. Jackson"), also known as "Samuel Jackson", "Samuel L. Jackson":
  - American publisher
  - has occupation clerk
  - has occupation publisher
  - has occupation stenographer
  - is instance of human
3. Samuel Lee Jackson (wd:Q131744796, matched via "Samuel L. Jackson"), also known as "Samuel Jackson", "Samuel L Jackson", "Samuel L. Jackson", "S L Jackson", "S. L. Jackson", etc.:
  - has occupation researcher
  - is instance of human
  - researcher
4. Samuel L. Jackson filmography (wd:Q4484398), also known as "filmography from Samuel L. Jackson", "filmography of Samuel L. Jackson":
  - Filmography of Samuel L. Jackson
  - is instance of filmography
5. Category:Samuel L. Jackson (wd:Q55271853):
  - is instance of Wikimedia category
  - Wikimedia category
6. Category:Films starring Samuel L. Jackson (wd:Q32815067):
  - is instance of Wikimedia category
  - Wikimedia category
7. Samuel L. Jackson/Ben Folds Five (wd:Q110036100), also known as "Saturday Night Live S23E10", "Saturday Night Live, season 23 episode 10":
  - episode of Saturday Night Live (S23 E10)
  - is instance of television series episode
8. Samuel Johnson, L. L. D. (wd:Q110038931):
  - is instance of mezzotint print
  - is instance of print
  - mezzotint - print made by William Doughty

content.message:
The most relevant entity for Samuel L. Jackson is "Samuel L. Jackson (wd:Q172678)" as it matches the known actor. I will now search for properties related to awards, specifically BAFTA, or nominations/wins, to find any awards he might have received in 1994 as a supporting actor. 

Next, I will search for properties related to "BAFTA" or "award" in Wikidata.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. based on (wd:P144 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "parody of"), also known as "adaptation of", "adapted from", "based on work", "based upon", "continuation of", etc.:
  - the work(s) or inputs used as the basis for subject item; for fictional analog use P1074
2. beam (wd:P2261 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "BOA"), also known as "BOA", "breadth", "width":
  - width of a ship at its widest point measured at its nominal waterline
3. main subject (wd:P921 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "of"), also known as "about", "artistic theme", "/common/topic/subject", "content deals with", "content describes", etc.:
  - primary topic of a work or act of communication
4. part of (wd:P361 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "of"), also known as "assembly of", "branch of", "chain", "collateral branch of", "component of", etc.:
  - object of which the subject is a part (if this subject is ... "has part" (P527, see also "has parts of the class" (P2670))
5. applies to jurisdiction (wd:P1001 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "of"), also known as "applicable area", "applicable country", "applicable geographic place", "applicable jurisdiction", "applicable location", etc.:
  - the item (institution, law, public office, public register, ... jurisdiction: a country, state, municipality, etc)
6. used by (wd:P1535 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "of"), also known as "armiger", "of", "played by", "user":
  - item or concept that makes use of the subject (use sub-properties when appropriate)
7. characteristic of (wd:P13044 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "of"), also known as "inheres in", "of", "property of", "quality of":
  - subject item or statement is a characteristic, quality, ... of this object (use a more specific property where possible)
8. class of object(s) of occurrence (wd:P12913 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "of"), also known as "acted on", "acts on", "affects item(s) of the class", "applies to item(s) of the class", "class of object of occurrence", etc.:
  - class that includes the object(s) to which this occurrence (or class of occurrence) occurs or occurred
9. object of occurrence (wd:P12912 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "of"), also known as "action applies to item", "affects", "did to", "direct object", "does to", etc.:
  - specific object to which an occurrence or class of occurrences applies
10. applies to part (wd:P518 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "of"), also known as "applied to", "applies to aspect", "applies to form", "applies to phase", "apply to", etc.:
  - part, aspect, or form of the item to which the claim applies

7. nominated for (wd:P1411 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "selected for award"), also known as "finalist for", "finalist for award", "have nomination to", "nominated for award", "nomination received", etc.:

9. award rationale (wd:P6208 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "award reason"), also known as "award citation", "award reason", "rationale":

10. funded by grant (wd:P11814 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "grant"), also known as "funder", "grant", "sponsor", "sponsored by grant":
